headline: Qualcomm Jumps 8%, Micron Pops 4% as AI Semiconductor Rally Enters Parabolic Phase
summary: Shares of Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) are up roughly 8% to around $237 in early Monday trading, extending one of the most aggressive multi-week runs in large-cap semis. Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) is climbing alongside it, up about 4% to around $774. The moves follow a blistering Friday session, when QCOM closed up 8% at $219.09 and MU ... headline: Micron stock surges 6% on Samsung strike concerns, $1000 target
summary: Investing.com -- Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) shares rose 5.75% Monday as labor unrest at Samsung Electronics and supply constraints drove optimism, with Deutsche Bank setting a $1,000 price target for the memory-chip maker, matching the Street high.

headline: This Week In AI Chips - NVIDIA Strengthens Board with Strategic Leadership Addition
summary: NVIDIA has announced the addition of Suzanne Nora Johnson to its board of directors, effective July 13, 2026. Her extensive leadership experience across finance, technology, and healthcare is anticipated to provide significant strategic insights for NVIDIA as it continues its advancements in AI and accelerated computing. Johnson's previous roles at Goldman Sachs and current board positions emphasize her expertise and potential contribution to NVIDIA's efforts in shaping the future of AI...

Johnson's previous roles at Goldman Sachs and current board positions emphasize her expertise and potential contribution to NVIDIA's efforts in shaping the future of AI...

headline: AI-Powered Chips Rally Rages On. Intel, Micron Stocks Pop.
summary: The AI-powered tech rally showed no signs of slowing down on Monday, as two red-hot chip stocks continued to surge. Micron Technology rose 2.8% as rival memory chip maker Samsung tried to avoid a general walkout.

content.title: AI-Powered Chips Rally Rages On. Intel, Micron Stocks Pop.
content.summary: The AI-powered tech rally showed no signs of slowing down on Monday, as two red-hot chip stocks continued to surge. Micron Technology rose 2.8% as rival memory chip maker Samsung tried to avoid a general walkout.
